title: Satya Nadella reveals Microsoft Build 2026 dates, flagship developer event shifts to San Francisco
summary: The annual Microsoft Build conference will take place on June 2-3 in San Francisco. The conference will see a keynote by the company's CEO Satya Nadella while a list of other eminent speakers are also confirmed.

title: From iPhone 17e to MacBook Air M5: Full list of Apple products launched this week
summary: Apple launched six new products this week including the iPhone 17e with A19 processor and the MacBook Pro and Air lineup. The company is expected to showcase these products for the first time to the press at different stores throughout the world.

title: Work or study on a budget? These 8 laptops under ₹50,000 deliver reliable performance
summary: From dependable everyday performance to reliable battery life, these laptops under ₹50,000 &nbsp;are suitable for students, professionals and remote workers.

title: Elon Musk's X cracks down on AI deepfakes during Israel-Iran war with new revenue sharing rules
summary: Elon Musk's platform X is revising its revenue-sharing programme and has warned creators about AI-generated content. The company says users posting AI videos of armed conflicts without disclosure will face 90-day suspensions from the programme, and subsequent violations will lead to permanent bans.

title: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok down? Thousands report receiving ‘Your account is currently unavailable’ message
summary: Facebook, TikTok and Instagram are down for users across the globe, with hundreds of people reporting outages. Users have reported receiving ‘Your account is currently unavailable due to a site issue’.

title: Apple hikes prices as iPhone maker unveils new MacBook Air and Pro versions — Check latest rates
summary: Apple has updated its MacBook Air and MacBook Pro with faster processors, upgraded storage, and new external monitors. Prices have increased, with the Air starting at $1,099 and the Pro at $2,199. The new models support advanced features such as Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 6.

title: Oppo K14 5G India launch date announced: Check expected price, specs and more
summary: The Oppo K14 5G is set to launch in India on March 9 at 12 noon. The phone will come with a 7,000 mAh battery ad support for 45W of fast charging. The device could come in the sub- ₹25,000 price bracket.&nbsp;

title: ‘Not worth it’: OpenAI researcher questions Pentagon amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend
summary: An OpenAI researcher has expressed concerns about the company's recent deal to deploy AI tools for the US Department of Defense. The company has been facing backlash since it announced the partnership with Pentagon last week.

title: Walmart-backed PhonePe targets up to $10.5 billion valuation in India IPO
summary: PhonePe is targeting a $9 billion to $10.5 billion valuation in its IPO, aiming to raise about $900 million to $1.05 billion. Backer Walmart will cut its stake, while Tiger Global and Microsoft plan to exit. The offering involves 50.7 million shares, with no new shares issued.

title: Meta, NewsCorp in AI content licensing deal worth up to $50 million a year
summary: Meta Platforms has agreed a multiyear AI content licensing deal with News Corp, reportedly worth up to $50 million annually. The three-year agreement allows Meta to use US and UK content for artificial-intelligence products and training. Chief executive Robert Thomson confirmed further negotiations

title: Head of Alibaba's Qwen AI division resigns
summary: The head of Alibaba Group’s Qwen AI model division, Lin Junyang, said he would step down, days after updated products were released. In a post on X, he wrote “Bye my beloved Qwen” without further explanation. The ⁠jump came after Chinese tech giants launched aggressive campaigns to draw more ​users

title: Sam Altman tells staff OpenAI has no say over Pentagon decisions
summary: OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman told employees the company does not get to decide how the Department of Defense (DoD) uses its artificial intelligence software. He said the Pentagon will hear OpenAI’s expertise but will not allow it to make operational decisions, following tensions with Anthropic.

title: AI contract restrictions could threaten military missions: US official
summary: A senior Pentagon official warned that broad operational limits in commercial AI contracts under the Biden administration could hinder real-time military planning and combat operations, with terms so restrictive that models might stop mid-mission. These concerns surfaced days after a row over the us

title: Anthropic nears $20 billion revenue run rate amid Pentagon feud
summary: Anthropic is nearing $20 billion in annual run-rate revenue, up sharply from late 2025, driven by strong adoption of its AI models and Claude Code. Valued at $380 billion, the company shows rapid growth, though its clash with the Pentagon now casts doubt over business prospects.

title: Intel board chair Frank Yeary to depart after 17 years
summary: Intel chair Frank Yeary will retire, with Craig Barratt set to succeed him. Yeary served on ⁠the board ‌since 2009 and was chair since 2023. He has presided over four CEO transitions and has dealt with ​the decline of Intel's ​manufacturing and the rise of TSMC.

title: State Department switches to OpenAI as US agencies start phasing out Anthropic
summary: United States cabinet agencies and the military ordered staff to stop using Anthropic products, including Claude, after failed talks over military safeguards. Departments will switch to OpenAI and Google models. The move follows President Donald Trump’s directive and affects Pentagon contracts.

title: OpenAI is developing alternative to Microsoft's GitHub: The Information
summary: OpenAI is developing a new code-hosting platform to rival GitHub after service disruptions. The early-stage project could be sold to customers, marking competition with Microsoft. A recent funding round valued OpenAI at $840 billion, showing strong AI investment interest.

title: Pentagon dispute bolsters Anthropic reputation but raises questions about AI readiness in military
summary: Anthropic refused to let Claude be used for autonomous weapons, prompting the Trump administration to ban it from government use. Downloads rose as consumers backed its ethics.
